data.narrative:
Heron Power is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Drew Baglino that has raised $170M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 6 same-sector, same-stage private companies including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $170M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage step-ups, yielding $323M. The peer-median method examines the 6 same-sector comparables, which show a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the climate tech space at this stage.

Synthesizing both methods produces a valuation range of $275M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $339M. This suggests fair value in the $330M–$350M band. Key uncertainties include the substantial spread within the peer set—Commonwealth Fusion Systems trades at a significant premium at $5.0B—and the lack of mature revenue multiples in early-stage climate tech, making comparable-company analysis inherently sensitive to stage timing and capital efficiency assumptions.
